lines.english: Lesson 33
lines.chinese: 第33课
lines.raw: Lesson 33 | 第33课

lines.english: A day to remember
lines.chinese: 值得纪念的一天
lines.raw: A day to remember | 值得纪念的一天

lines.english: What incident began the series of traffic accidents?
lines.chinese: 是什么事件引发了这一系列交通事故？
lines.raw: What incident began the series of traffic accidents? | 是什么事件引发了这一系列交通事故？

lines.english: We have all experienced days when everything goes wrong.
lines.chinese: 我们都经历过事事不顺的日子。
lines.raw: We have all experienced days when everything goes wrong. | 我们都经历过事事不顺的日子。

lines.english: A day may begin well enough, but suddenly everything seems to get out of control.
lines.chinese: 一天也许开始得很好，但突然间一切似乎都失去了控制。
lines.raw: A day may begin well enough, but suddenly everything seems to get out of control. | 一天也许开始得很好，但突然间一切似乎都失去了控制。

lines.english: What invariably happens is that a great number of things choose to go wrong at precisely the same moment.
lines.chinese: 事情总是这样，很多事情偏偏选择在同一时刻出错。
lines.raw: What invariably happens is that a great number of things choose to go wrong at precisely the same moment. | 事情总是这样，很多事情偏偏选择在同一时刻出错。

lines.english: It is as if a single unimportant event set up a chain of reactions.
lines.chinese: 仿佛一个微不足道的事件引发了一连串的连锁反应。
lines.raw: It is as if a single unimportant event set up a chain of reactions. | 仿佛一个微不足道的事件引发了一连串的连锁反应。

lines.english: Let us suppose that you are preparing a meal and keeping an eye on the baby at the same time.
lines.chinese: 假设你正在准备饭菜，同时还要照看婴儿。
lines.raw: Let us suppose that you are preparing a meal and keeping an eye on the baby at the same time. | 假设你正在准备饭菜，同时还要照看婴儿。

lines.english: The telephone rings and this marks the prelude to an unforeseen series of catastrophes.
lines.chinese: 电话铃响了，这预示着一系列意想不到的灾难即将发生。
lines.raw: The telephone rings and this marks the prelude to an unforeseen series of catastrophes. | 电话铃响了，这预示着一系列意想不到的灾难即将发生。

lines.english: While you are on the phone, the baby pulls the tablecloth off the table,
lines.chinese: 当你打电话的时候，婴儿把桌布从桌子上扯了下来，
lines.raw: While you are on the phone, the baby pulls the tablecloth off the table, | 当你打电话的时候，婴儿把桌布从桌子上扯了下来，

lines.english: smashing half your best crockery and cutting himself in the process.
lines.chinese: 打碎了你一半最好的餐具，还把自己划伤了。
lines.raw: smashing half your best crockery and cutting himself in the process. | 打碎了你一半最好的餐具，还把自己划伤了。

lines.english: You hang up hurriedly and attend to baby, crockery, etc.
lines.chinese: 你急忙挂断电话，去照看婴儿、餐具等。
lines.raw: You hang up hurriedly and attend to baby, crockery, etc. | 你急忙挂断电话，去照看婴儿、餐具等。

lines.english: Meanwhile, the meal gets burnt.
lines.chinese: 与此同时，饭菜烧糊了。
lines.raw: Meanwhile, the meal gets burnt. | 与此同时，饭菜烧糊了。

lines.english: As if this were not enough to reduce you to tears, your husband arrives, unexpectedly bringing three guests to dinner.
lines.chinese: 好像这些还不足以让你落泪，你的丈夫又回来了，出乎意料地带了三位客人来吃晚饭。
lines.raw: As if this were not enough to reduce you to tears, your husband arrives, unexpectedly bringing three guests to dinner. | 好像这些还不足以让你落泪，你的丈夫又回来了，出乎意料地带了三位客人来吃晚饭。

lines.english: Things can go wrong on a big scale as a number of people recently discovered in Parramatta, a suburb of Sydney.
lines.chinese: 事情也会大规模地出错，正如最近在悉尼郊区帕拉马塔的一些人所发现的那样。
lines.raw: Things can go wrong on a big scale as a number of people recently discovered in Parramatta, a suburb of Sydney. | 事情也会大规模地出错，正如最近在悉尼郊区帕拉马塔的一些人所发现的那样。

lines.english: During the rush hour one evening two cars collided and both drivers began to argue.
lines.chinese: 一天晚上，在交通高峰期，两辆汽车相撞，两位司机开始争吵起来。
lines.raw: During the rush hour one evening two cars collided and both drivers began to argue. | 一天晚上，在交通高峰期，两辆汽车相撞，两位司机开始争吵起来。

lines.english: The woman immediately behind the two cars happened to be a learner.
lines.chinese: 紧跟在两辆车后面的一位女士碰巧是位新手司机。
lines.raw: The woman immediately behind the two cars happened to be a learner. | 紧跟在两辆车后面的一位女士碰巧是位新手司机。

lines.english: She suddenly got into a panic and stopped her car.
lines.chinese: 她突然惊慌失措，把车停了下来。
lines.raw: She suddenly got into a panic and stopped her car. | 她突然惊慌失措，把车停了下来。

lines.english: This made the driver following her brake hard.
lines.chinese: 这使得跟在她后面的司机猛踩刹车。
lines.raw: This made the driver following her brake hard. | 这使得跟在她后面的司机猛踩刹车。

lines.english: His wife was sitting beside him holding a large cake.
lines.chinese: 他的妻子坐在他旁边，手里拿着一个大蛋糕。
lines.raw: His wife was sitting beside him holding a large cake. | 他的妻子坐在他旁边，手里拿着一个大蛋糕。

lines.english: As she was thrown forward,
lines.chinese: 当她向前扑去时，
lines.raw: As she was thrown forward, | 当她向前扑去时，

lines.english: the cake went right through the windscreen and landed on the road.
lines.chinese: 蛋糕直接穿过挡风玻璃，落在了路上。
lines.raw: the cake went right through the windscreen and landed on the road. | 蛋糕直接穿过挡风玻璃，落在了路上。

lines.english: Seeing a cake flying through the air,
lines.chinese: 看到一个蛋糕飞过空中，
lines.raw: Seeing a cake flying through the air, | 看到一个蛋糕飞过空中，

lines.english: a lorry driver who was drawing up alongside the car, pulled up all of a sudden.
lines.chinese: 一位正把车开到汽车旁边的货车司机，突然把车停了下来。
lines.raw: a lorry driver who was drawing up alongside the car, pulled up all of a sudden. | 一位正把车开到汽车旁边的货车司机，突然把车停了下来。

lines.english: The lorry was loaded with empty beer bottles and hundreds of them slid off the back of the vehicle and on to the road.
lines.chinese: 货车上装满了空啤酒瓶，几百个瓶子从车后部滑落到路上。
lines.raw: The lorry was loaded with empty beer bottles and hundreds of them slid off the back of the vehicle and on to the road. | 货车上装满了空啤酒瓶，几百个瓶子从车后部滑落到路上。

lines.english: This led to yet another angry argument.
lines.chinese: 这又引发了另一场激烈的争吵。
lines.raw: This led to yet another angry argument. | 这又引发了另一场激烈的争吵。

lines.english: Meanwhile, the traffic piled up behind.
lines.chinese: 与此同时，后面的交通堵塞了。
lines.raw: Meanwhile, the traffic piled up behind. | 与此同时，后面的交通堵塞了。

lines.english: It took the police nearly an hour to get the traffic on the move again.
lines.chinese: 警察花了将近一个小时才让交通再次恢复通行。
lines.raw: It took the police nearly an hour to get the traffic on the move again. | 警察花了将近一个小时才让交通再次恢复通行。

lines.english: In the meantime, the lorry driver had to sweep up hundreds of broken bottles.
lines.chinese: 与此同时，货车司机不得不清扫数百个碎瓶子。
lines.raw: In the meantime, the lorry driver had to sweep up hundreds of broken bottles. | 与此同时，货车司机不得不清扫数百个碎瓶子。

lines.english: Only two stray dogs benefited from all this confusion, for they greedily devoured what was left of the cake.
lines.chinese: 只有两只流浪狗从这场混乱中受益，因为它们贪婪地吞食了剩下的蛋糕。
lines.raw: Only two stray dogs benefited from all this confusion, for they greedily devoured what was left of the cake. | 只有两只流浪狗从这场混乱中受益，因为它们贪婪地吞食了剩下的蛋糕。

lines.english: It was just one of those days!
lines.chinese: 真是倒霉的一天！
lines.raw: It was just one of those days! | 真是倒霉的一天！
